title: tax — validation rules
description: Custom Laravel validation rules published by TaxServiceProvider::boot(). All rules are used both by the observer chain (for authoritative refusal on write) AND by FormRequest data classes (for early request-time validation with clear error surfaces).

rules.name: valid_percentage
rules.description: Decimal in range [0.0000, 1.0000]. Rejects values above 1.0 (100%+ tax rates are almost always misconfigurations). Precision to 4 decimal places (matches DB column shape decimal(5,4)).
rules.default_message: The :attribute must be a decimal between 0.0000 and 1.0000 (representing 0% to 100%).

rules.name: valid_country_code
rules.description: Value is a valid ISO 3166-1 alpha-2 country code. Backed by league/iso3166 catalog (offline lookup).
rules.default_message: The :attribute must be a valid ISO 3166-1 alpha-2 country code (e.g. US, GB, DE, JP).

rules.name: valid_state_code
rules.description: Value is a valid ISO 3166-2 subdivision code for the provided country_code. Requires country_code as a parameter or from the same form.
rules.default_message: The :attribute must be a valid ISO 3166-2 subdivision code for country ':country_code'.

rules.name: valid_postal_pattern
rules.description: Value is a valid PCRE regex. Tests compilation by attempting @preg_match($pattern, '') and checking for errors.
rules.default_message: The :attribute must be a valid PCRE regex pattern.

rules.name: valid_iso_currency
rules.description: ISO 4217 3-letter uppercase currency code. Skipped when the parent field (e.g. source_currency on tax_calculations) is null.
rules.default_message: The :attribute must be a valid ISO 4217 currency code (e.g. USD, EUR, GBP, JPY).

rules.name: valid_positive_amount
rules.description: Integer >= 0 in cents. NOTE: unlike some finance rules, tax allows negative amounts on refund calculations — those are validated by a distinct rule. This rule is for CREATE paths (rates, jurisdictions) where negatives are always wrong.
rules.default_message: The :attribute must be a non-negative integer (in cents).

rules.name: valid_effective_range
rules.description: For fields (effective_from, effective_to) or (valid_from, valid_until): the start date must be strictly less than the end date when both are set. Either side null is OK.
rules.default_message: The :attribute range is invalid: start date must be before end date.

rules.name: valid_provider_config
rules.description: config jsonb validates against the per-provider JSON Schema in data/providers/<provider>-config.schema.json. TaxJar: api_token. Avalara: account_id + license_key + company_code. Stripe Tax: api_key. NativeCalculator: {} (empty — no external credentials). Custom Webhook: webhook_url + hmac_secret.
rules.default_message: The provider config does not match the schema for the ':provider' provider. Missing required fields: :missing_fields.

rules.name: valid_exemption_certificate_url
rules.description: certificate_document_url must (a) start with 'https://', (b) resolve to the tenant's S3 bucket prefix (e.g. https://stackra-certificates.s3.amazonaws.com/tenants/{tenant_short_id}/...), (c) NOT contain query parameters that expose the underlying signing key (only tenant-scoped signed URL params allowed).
rules.default_message: The :attribute must be an HTTPS S3 signed URL under the tenant's scoped bucket prefix.

rules.name: valid_verification_status_transition
rules.description: verification_status state machine. Valid transitions: pending → verified (with verified_by_user_id set OR is_automatic_registry_check=true); pending → rejected (with rejection_reason set); verified → expired (automatic only, via ExpireExemptionsJob). No transitions FROM verified back to pending. No un-reject. No un-expire.
rules.default_message: The verification_status transition from ':old' to ':new' is not allowed.
